    93.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Astarion has a certain swagger, don't you find? An interesting fellow. Nice hair, to boot.TG_ORI_Astarion_Recruited!TG_ORI_Astarion_Recruited

    19.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Githyanki are impressive - cold as ice, when they're not blazing hot. I wager Lae'zel's no exception.TG_ORI_Laezel_Recruited!TG_ORI_Laezel_Recruited

    270.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Mysterious, that Shadowheart - and mysteriously endearing. Behind those eyes lurk uncountable secrets.TG_ORI_ShadowheartRecruited!TG_ORI_ShadowheartRecruited

    • +
      72.Origin_Intro_Wyll: An old fairy tale my father read to me. The kind with a hero, a villain, and a moral.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 2aa35cdf-7f39-1d66-04b9-aa404d473f9aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hd971ba17gb0dcg4299g8eabg8c875b0be48aText UUID: 7bd2b4c8-a880-469c-9127-69decaa2d6e0Group ID: 41cbe586-2181-91e9-d405-7ab1fe0d9a30Group Index: 1
      • +
        96.Origin_Intro_Wyll: A farmer made a deal with a devil, so the story goes: in exchange for the farmer's dearest fruit, the devil granted him a bottomless coinpurse.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 3cb8e319-8a18-1be3-a791-1b72a25224fcSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h637faca0gb1b2g4c16g86eag11b25b9c6d62Text UUID: 6cbe0994-d348-45a4-8358-13badcf5fbceGroup ID: 41cbe586-2181-91e9-d405-7ab1fe0d9a30Group Index: 2
        • +
          413.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The farmer's dearest fruit, naturally, was no apple nor peach, but his beloved daughter.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: f962acf0-0eb8-0ef5-5532-7f2890bd5172Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hf5fe7f96g0bb3g4cb0g90a8g489be6f1aac7Text UUID: 9f149fca-054c-4019-913f-d36dc93a9bebGroup ID: 41cbe586-2181-91e9-d405-7ab1fe0d9a30Group Index: 3
          • +
            238.Origin_Intro_Wyll: We can learn a lot from fairy tales, don't you think?

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 89e75ba9-1ff1-3734-7b2a-56ad1a87d8e1Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h3e1c900eg3fccg43ffgbf7fg57dbf7518ec6Text UUID: e19015d9-e9ca-4fe8-a3f9-0d9f7c5192abGroup ID: 41cbe586-2181-91e9-d405-7ab1fe0d9a30Group Index: 4
            • +
              12.Player: With the right teacher, yes. What are your thoughts on the devil, Wyll?

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 088c4c11-424f-4ea9-19d5-6f9cc8be079aSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hae295932g7697g453cg858fgb876ab18e864Text UUID: 5e3b180f-e8b0-4e73-bcbd-38353729ebbdShow Once: True
              • +
                327.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Refuse him, no matter how tempting the offer, no matter how delicious the feast he lays out for you. The cost's always too great.

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c2c0d64d-f738-e406-fcff-82e3499cc31fSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h02712883g1defg45c6gae4cgd0f242dd8ffdText UUID: e491e942-da57-4d61-bb31-8773a0f8392dTimeline Phase Index: 153Timeline Dialog Node UUID: c2c0d64d-f738-e406-fcff-82e3499cc31fTimeline Phase Duration: 9.63sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1629.29s-1638.61s] | @ 1630.49s | speaker 0 | Angry (8) | variation 1Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1629.29s-1638.61s] | @ 1632.72s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [1629.29s-1638.61s] | @ 1634.5s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [1629.29s-1638.61s] | @ 1636.39s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)
                • +
                  2.Player: Don't worry. I have no interest in a devil's deal.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 00c72405-6a55-75cc-0003-3e6cd21552c7Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h7306b028g25a6g4fa0g85aegf8728d0082e6Text UUID: 118229b1-f529-4989-bc01-140bc13dd6f0Show Once: TrueApproval Rating: 5c0534d8-70d1-45bc-8fb4-53b277d1af87
                  • +
                    125.Origin_Intro_Wyll: That's because you still have hope. But when he becomes your last hope, remember this.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 48f28c0b-1d45-474e-25ab-d341b32f02e5Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h75fda422gdfdag4571gbeabgcb20f7f67beeText UUID: 6cd3599e-c470-4692-821f-ef9fa5be23f3Timeline Phase Index: 170Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 48f28c0b-1d45-474e-25ab-d341b32f02e5Timeline Phase Duration: 6.56sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1823.6s-1830.16s] | @ 1825.94s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1823.6s-1830.16s] | @ 1827.52s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [1830.16s-1834.98s] | @ 1830.16s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)
                    • +
                      336.Origin_Intro_Wyll: He'll require of you only what you're least ready to part with.

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c97bfb0d-238c-771c-d596-e9a266aadf13Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h310fc9ecgf284g45e7g989fge31f64c59ee2Text UUID: 28e4db36-8626-4bc4-9541-c4f5a8e1cddfTimeline Phase Index: 180Timeline Dialog Node UUID: c97bfb0d-238c-771c-d596-e9a266aadf13Timeline Phase Duration: 8.26sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1907.05s-1915.31s] | @ 1912.36s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Group ID: c97bfb0d-238c-771c-d596-e9a266aadf13Group Index: 0
                      • +
                        253.Origin_Intro_Wyll: And then require more still.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 91726193-512f-3008-5db0-cb1c21e52375Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h2e797804gef12g4b43gbea2g6a5d5edfd161Text UUID: a283bfe6-2594-4738-b601-b27fba431826Group ID: c97bfb0d-238c-771c-d596-e9a266aadf13Group Index: 1
                        • +
                          255.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You might think you'd give up anything for a cure.

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 92981eef-b79e-55f1-c26f-bf30cefc8edbSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hbc816653gbca3g4e02gb593gaf0e29ac4759Text UUID: abb6f0a0-4fdc-4555-8759-4f5d351cf260Timeline Phase Index: 176Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 92981eef-b79e-55f1-c26f-bf30cefc8edbTimeline Phase Duration: 9.51sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 2 [1880.34s-1885.88s] | @ 1881.47s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 2 [1880.34s-1885.88s] | @ 1883.68s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [1885.88s-1895.22s] | @ 1885.88s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Group ID: 92981eef-b79e-55f1-c26f-bf30cefc8edbGroup Index: 0
                          • +
                            220.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But the devil won't take just anything. He'll take everything.

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8020af89-b7c6-2d8c-7e24-dcac1d11dd19Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h8da129d5g47fag43e6g850fg1a43b6275c3cText UUID: 79753dbf-b355-41c2-aaac-b9e47d343450Group ID: 92981eef-b79e-55f1-c26f-bf30cefc8edbGroup Index: 1

                • +
                  273.Player: He spoke of a cure. We have to at least hear him out.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 9c8d32c6-8226-f05d-725c-c4d3f21f4723Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hb7c9bf7dg9496g4aa2ga5a7ge95c96ed278bText UUID: adcc49b8-6c82-487e-83b4-6422f9ba3825Show Once: TrueApproval Rating: f9f9e333-8e0f-4293-826d-374386c3b18f
                  • +
                    384.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Take a single step towards him, and he'll dog you the whole journey.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e7e6f8ef-afd9-53fc-2283-2a60a11ced9bSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h172c28d2g22acg442cgb9d9ge92d530df05fText UUID: 48316792-97d0-41de-8f5a-392f88065f41Timeline Phase Index: 173Timeline Dialog Node UUID: e7e6f8ef-afd9-53fc-2283-2a60a11ced9bTimeline Phase Duration: 4.29sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1850.04s-1854.33s] | @ 1851.36s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1854.33s-1862.99s] | @ 1854.33s | speaker 2 | Fear (16)

                    89.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I made a mistake. This is your chance to learn from it.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 35adba5e-2d4a-acc2-5108-284760a2fa58Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hd1695734ge94dg4c59gb99fg69c6b843e730Text UUID: 4134c644-1e98-4cf5-9b19-d57c604d0e8aTimeline Phase Index: 155Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 35adba5e-2d4a-acc2-5108-284760a2fa58Timeline Phase Duration: 4.25sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1649.41s-1653.66s] | @ 1650.98s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1653.66s-1660.76s] | @ 1653.66s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)

                      88.Origin_Intro_Wyll: A devil wouldn't bargain if he thought there was no chance of success, Lae'zel.

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 353e4328-17eb-cc77-529e-b9106fab9e1bSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: he35def37g988eg4c9aga6cdgcc59bb4010e0Text UUID: dbe5fa41-c0e5-4e22-aabb-67eda085b83fGroup ID: 29ea2e92-119f-6f62-ad5d-a5ae35eabac1Group Index: 1
                      • +

                377.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Do not trust a devil. Is that plain enough?

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e3d076a0-9226-3965-c0bb-7df10f63968aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h434b19e2g69bdg4fa7gaa69g00cd49469d70Text UUID: cfe89059-a724-44a9-90d2-c0944251fa94Timeline Phase Index: 178Timeline Dialog Node UUID: e3d076a0-9226-3965-c0bb-7df10f63968aTimeline Phase Duration: 4.23sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1895.22s-1899.45s] | @ 1896.25s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1895.22s-1899.45s] | @ 1898.13s | speaker 0 | Angry (8)
                • See Node 327 (Origin_Intro_Wyll) Refuse him, no matter how tempting the offer, no matter how delicious the feast he lays out for you. The cost's always too great.
            • +

    38.Origin_Intro_Wyll: An impostor in our camp? We must identify them - and quickly. Without trust, our fellowship is doomed to wither and die.TG_WYR_Gortash_WarnedImpostor!TG_WYR_Gortash_WarnedImpostor

    47.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The gith knight is as merciless as the dragon he rides. That we still have our heads is nothing short of a miracle.TG_PLA_GithChokepoint_EncounteredGith!TG_PLA_GithChokepoint_EncounteredGith

      115.Origin_Intro_Wyll: And it seems Mystra's offered you an equally explosive solution. A shame my first brush with the famed Elminster couldn't be a tad more... optimistic.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 4454e1dc-2a02-6215-bd27-86a648253fd2Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h7b521d58g09a8g4a45g87eag63e00e66ba6cText UUID: 27c38776-1653-49a8-bccf-0218c8b0f29aGroup ID: 627e38b4-c986-3677-3440-9e23c3b5f1beGroup Index: 1
      • +
        167.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Listen, Gale - I might invoke the Triad from time to time, appeal to Helm. But I'm no man of faith. Not like you.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h7982ba7ag28bfg4d8bgab47g48ce285ca3e8Text UUID: 1738a299-e35c-46e8-a6a7-3584d7587acfTimeline Phase Index: 188Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aTimeline Phase Duration: 38.7sTimeline Phase Shots: 5Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [2011.78s-2017.68s] | @ 2012.75s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 2 [2017.68s-2030.02s] | @ 2019.6s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 2 [2017.68s-2030.02s] | @ 2021.48s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 2 [2017.68s-2030.02s] | @ 2024.36s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 5: Shot 2 [2017.68s-2030.02s] | @ 2026.4s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 6: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2032.44s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 7: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2033.96s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 8: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2036.66s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 9: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2039.97s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 10: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2041.5s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 11: Shot 4 [2032.44s-2044.28s] | @ 2043.12s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 12: Shot 5 [2044.28s-2050.48s] | @ 2044.32s | speaker 0 | Angry (8)Timeline Emotion 13: Shot 5 [2044.28s-2050.48s] | @ 2046.46s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 14: Shot 5 [2044.28s-2050.48s] | @ 2047.68s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 15: Shot 1 [2050.48s-2053.94s] | @ 2050.48s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Group ID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aGroup Index: 0

          211.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I don't know what drives a man to consider his own death, among countless others, to be an appropriate exchange for his goddess' forgiveness.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7b60916f-95cf-fd1e-15e9-6367e9c75de9Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hd56e6129g6155g4417ga31bg77c58c6b93dbText UUID: a38fe159-1323-4e36-b2b5-116ee916bcb0Group ID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aGroup Index: 1
          • +
            324.Origin_Intro_Wyll: To me, it all sounds like nonsense.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c1af8640-c906-2566-433a-88e5404c039aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h419223fdgc65dg4be2g97f2g4a14d040cad5Text UUID: 1a0665de-648d-447a-ac14-bdf5c9cad6a7Group ID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aGroup Index: 2
            • +
              106.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The faith that matters is that which you hold in yourself, in the ones that most matter to you.

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 40daa730-54b9-4a2b-67de-13af01c6d058Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h626d6176ga550g4a3cga4e8gfeb1175130b2Text UUID: c720e47a-6a53-437b-af0d-97a273bee4f1Group ID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aGroup Index: 3
              • +
                32.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Big bomb be damned, you've got everything you need to defeat the Absolute already: Talent, nerve, and powerful allies at your side.

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 131ed12a-9fc5-2b03-f2e8-21d2300cf3a9Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: ha9f8ef6dg714cg46aag8fbfg437308a45001Text UUID: 43336cab-06d8-4998-a53d-7d3447758ee4Group ID: 63b2b7d1-d67b-1a50-d563-5a991e21b78aGroup Index: 4
                • •

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: dcf9e4ba-21fa-13c6-66c9-f860e0e775b4Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h6ba9ace3gc465g40e3g8d71g06663f1f63acText UUID: 30ec69d3-65c3-4659-a1c0-ce867050c1f6Group ID: 2a69242c-4b38-8234-1c12-10bc8f162188Group Index: 1
          • +
            178.Origin_Intro_Wyll: To me, it all sounds like nonsense.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 69f0f966-a5b1-88d4-31af-12bdb9cb33c7Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h44e7579ag20f9g42c0g8881gf2fb9d8d2034Text UUID: 105b1edc-b101-4720-a92a-478e6bc30a90Group ID: 2a69242c-4b38-8234-1c12-10bc8f162188Group Index: 2
            • +
              23.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The faith that matters is that which you hold in yourself, in the ones that most matter to you.

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0ce019cb-b4b4-6633-7957-4ce9db96c572Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hcc0eb634gff07g4b76g9d1eg502cb58973d8Text UUID: cfea02ef-acfe-4ea8-982b-bc089331455cGroup ID: 2a69242c-4b38-8234-1c12-10bc8f162188Group Index: 3
              • +
                81.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Big bomb be damned, Gale's got everything he needs to defeat the Absolute already: Talent, nerve, and powerful allies at his side.

                Metadata

                • •

      154.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The Beloved Ranger. I - I can't believe it. I grew up hearing all about your brave deeds. And Boo's, of course.MinscWyll_InParty2_Event_TalkedToMinsc

      Flags and Tags

      checktype Tag | speaker 2 Speaker 2

                              149.Speaker 2: Blade of Smiting Evil Frontiers...? Eh. If it is all the same, Minsc and Boo will just call you Wyll.

              181.Player: And you then you pacted with a half-devil. What do you think of that, Minsc?

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 6bac39f4-03a1-9fea-6f73-b0464f5bb115Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h8d0e21c9g0cefg41fegbe62g931282d7c1fdText UUID: d57fae0b-2c12-4f6c-b638-162c26e4b521Show Once: True
              • +
                139.Speaker 2: I broke bread and evil bones with a child of Bhaal. Minsc is not so judgey as you, my friend, so long as Wyll's deed was done for good.

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 54bc9c72-2117-ab4b-60b4-cb78ab4742e8Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h707d2d57gf843g4954g82c3gd5dd67f4cd15Text UUID: 74dcf0a8-1c8f-42d3-a516-35cdabf132c9Timeline Phase Index: 159Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 54bc9c72-2117-ab4b-60b4-cb78ab4742e8Timeline Phase Duration: 16.92sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1685.26s | speaker 2 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1686.16s | speaker 2 | Angry (8)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1687.73s | speaker 2 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 4: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1689.43s | speaker 2 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 5: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1692.06s | speaker 2 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 6: Shot 1 [1684.09s-1695.75s] | @ 1693.76s | speaker 2 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 7: Shot 2 [1695.75s-1701.01s] | @ 1695.75s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 8: Shot 2 [1695.75s-1701.01s] | @ 1697.09s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 9: Shot 2 [1695.75s-1701.01s] | @ 1699.34s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 10: Shot 1 [1701.01s-1708.17s] | @ 1701.01s | speaker 2 | Fear (16)Group ID: 54bc9c72-2117-ab4b-60b4-cb78ab4742e8Group Index: 0

    • +
      124.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Listen to me. I knew another like you - Gorion's Ward, one of Baldur's Gate's great heroes. Bhaal's blood ran through their veins too.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 48cb2e11-b08e-1961-42a3-29c40320d2aaSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h82b3f1a9g0c64g4cb2ga2d1gc7237e13be3cText UUID: 1146cb05-1f8a-457f-a5a8-853168b701aaGroup ID: 0341429f-d6aa-475c-2f9f-bc805d47eee0Group Index: 1
      • +
        261.Origin_Intro_Wyll: They burned away their own darkness with their own inner light. They chose courage, they chose honour - and so can you.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 96335de3-3735-6c40-ecde-9dba2173e529Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: ha50db41eg545cg47dfg97a4g68c9d86025c1Text UUID: 6e7e56d0-6fe0-4921-8e65-90a20c09c273Group ID: 0341429f-d6aa-475c-2f9f-bc805d47eee0Group Index: 2
        • •

    110.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You have conquered your urge, you have taken back your own soul. I'm so proud of you.TG_DarkUrge_DefiedBhaalORI_DarkUrge_Knows_Cured!TG_DarkUrge_DefiedBhaal

    355.Origin_Intro_Wyll: By the gone gods. It isn't the blood you've spilled or the lives you've snuffed out that horrify me most.TG_DarkUrge_AcceptedBhaal!TG_DarkUrge_AcceptedBhaal

      83.Origin_Intro_Wyll: It's the pride you take in it, as if you've done all sixteen planes a great favour.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 31efd809-94a3-cea3-4893-900627fb2016Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h1e08fa4dg4e98g40b6g9289g4653695a53d5Text UUID: e5498419-b250-4322-9059-4349abf605f8Group ID: d52dfb7f-4d08-8c25-39e0-4aa7cb29a8f6Group Index: 1
      • •
        92.Origin_Intro_Wyll: When the Absolute falls, I wager our alliance falls with it. [End]

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 39d6bad2-f3ac-e315-a354-ee8b2adc0d00Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h0f1879f3g2d7bg405bgb36eg8e69ac312684Text UUID: 2805157e-c9f2-4b1e-93c1-f8249abebb23End Node: TrueGroup ID: d52dfb7f-4d08-8c25-39e0-4aa7cb29a8f6Group Index: 2
  • -
    177.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Father taught me to come to terms with my mortality. Mizora forced me to come to terms with a hellish fate.TG_DarkUrge_FailedBhaal!TG_DarkUrge_FailedBhaal

    • +
      186.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But to be destined for madness? I don't know if I could ever make peace with it.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 6d9d77b6-820d-5ab1-0fa4-a0af395c5bdaSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hdd0a30dag90f6g49adgab33g0acfff87d61aText UUID: c8955149-ab4f-4d8c-80fd-575211ecac3bGroup ID: 69832fae-9176-7e05-61de-b4addd982a9bGroup Index: 1
      • •
        312.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I don't envy you. But for what it's worth, I'm still at your side. And for now, I'm not going anywhere. [End]

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: bb771e20-3df1-208c-cac5-52c01abb42ffSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h4efb2297gcaf5g49e3gb97fg443ec78bb698Text UUID: 5f3988e4-d2ac-48d0-b006-c907414ee80fEnd Node: TrueGroup ID: 69832fae-9176-7e05-61de-b4addd982a9bGroup Index: 2

                179.Origin_Intro_Wyll: And now you carry doom itself within you as penance. My father would lecture you on the perils of power-lust -

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 6a67d9a1-df52-fb82-c3f9-34bdfbea9222Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h0c8d2105gd7edg42eegbc62g9c73ce941fb4Text UUID: 11eae0ab-57e2-4fba-93ff-2c8fcd48ea06Group ID: fa1503be-cfde-f605-55a6-c1345dececb8Group Index: 1
                • +
                  4.Origin_Intro_Wyll: - but that's not a lesson that needs repeating, I wager.

      373.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Her mind will be wiped, and Viconia will remake her in whatever image she desires.

        251.Origin_Intro_Wyll: A cruel fate, and for what? An unreliable ally in Viconia? Or just for the sheer cruelty of it?

    219.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Dammit all, we've got to get Gale back. If that means killing Gortash, nothing would give me greater pleasure.!GLO_Gortash_State_PermaDefeatedTG_GEN_OrinAbducted_Gale!TG_GEN_OrinAbducted_Gale

    68.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I hope that's the end of this silly quarrel.       354.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Careful who you point your blade at next. We can afford no more unrest among us, not if we're to survive long enough to rid ourselves of these worms. [End]Lae'zel

      Flags and Tags

      ruletagtype rule
      • Lae'zel {b5682d1d-c395-489c-9675-1f9b0c328ea5}

      Text

      Variant 1

      combine=0
      • Lae'zel
      Careful who you point your blade at next. We can afford no more unrest among us, not if we're to survive long enough to rid ourselves of these worms.
      hdec8f58ege2d5g4389gb94dgddae42ab3dee | 2e76d96e-e412-428d-ae06-67b10c479d72 | stub

      Variant 2

      If she points a blade at one of us again, don't hesitate to strike. We can afford no more unrest among us, not if we're to survive long enough to rid ourselves of these worms.

      346.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I spent years swatting away my fears and doubts of my infernal fate - but I eventually made my peace. I had to.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: cfb8fe7b-5359-9740-8f0d-0b8a4381223cSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hcdce6426gdf24g436fga0c8gc99b5c3aa860Text UUID: 7ff378aa-8d9d-49f6-9011-609d17797c60Group ID: cadf1b00-d030-cdd1-e2c0-fd0e3c1541dbGroup Index: 1
      • •
        105.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You will too, Karlach. I know it. Until then, I'm here every step of the way. [End]Approval_AtLeast_10_For_Sp1

      65.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But you said it anyway, to your immense credit. You're choosing truth, you're choosing freedom - for you, for Orpheus, for your people.Lae'zel

      236.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I know better than to be disappointed in you - that ship has long sailed the Sea of Swords.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8855b8ab-e51f-5be1-eba2-666000323da0Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h773fa3f7g322dg4258g80e1gab756cd52833Text UUID: 2b2d6861-4078-4e3f-aca8-75dd8870afc8Group ID: 79c8fa97-b21f-4485-d67d-38ee674efa84Group Index: 1
      • •
        210.Origin_Intro_Wyll: No - I'm disappointed in me. For believing you were any different than what you always showed yourself to be. [End]

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7af4fbbd-d753-4b5f-b857-8d9d177acec9Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hfe484360g974ag4f88g9ea1g0e2dd629e383Text UUID: 3a19a746-b74e-4dd5-bc9b-b3a07ba58bf4End Node: TrueGroup ID: 79c8fa97-b21f-4485-d67d-38ee674efa84Group Index: 2
  • -
    310.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I've known wizards of Lorroakan's ilk. So sure of ignorance in everyone else, they can't fathom being fooled themselves.TG_LOW_SorcerousSundries_PlayersConvincedNSIsDead!TG_LOW_SorcerousSundries_PlayersConvincedNSIsDead

      197.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But I think you've got the right idea. Better I live fully and meaningfully than to pine for an always-and-forever I could never achieve. [End]

      144.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Though I suppose that's not what you wanted to talk about. [End]

      Metadata

  • -
    225.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I had a dream last night - a vivid one. And so did you, judging from the way my tadpole is tingling.TG_Daisy_AfterDream1!TG_Daisy_AfterDream1

    Flags and Tags

    • +
      160.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Someone came to me and promised to protect me while claiming that the parasite could empower me.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 5f9897f0-51a5-9c37-c226-8ef4858111d3Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h97612f3agbcadg44d5gac2bg7f89aa653e6eText UUID: 006aab7c-b904-47f0-87e9-da2288dbfb91Group ID: 82b15ea4-81ac-2625-6b56-86fc66eaf513Group Index: 1
      • +
        241.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Tempting as these powers sound, we should recognise this dream for what it is - the tadpoles' little trick.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8c431201-2fe1-382c-9076-9c3f4bb6964cSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h9fbd88fcg7f21g4eaegbba1g76c9451afed5Text UUID: f0cc4408-0c0e-48e4-842e-643a7f78bcaaGroup ID: 82b15ea4-81ac-2625-6b56-86fc66eaf513Group Index: 2
        • +
          151.Origin_Intro_Wyll: No good ever came from trusting honey-tongued strangers conjured up by illithid worms.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 5b1e05d8-14e9-fe55-cf8b-014cd31fdf1eSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h32bfee6cg4b59g4646gb4fcg0621fd283b00Text UUID: f8a5bf7d-b42c-4e84-b15b-61c61faf6865Group ID: 82b15ea4-81ac-2625-6b56-86fc66eaf513Group Index: 3
          • +
            326.Player: I think we should hear out this dream figure. Maybe these 'powers' could be useful.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: c2361bf1-842d-34e9-698a-355f58f4669dSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hf5688945g74f8g4f57gb395g23fa5f6d36ceText UUID: 9cb4705b-2706-4a4f-bcb0-8a55fca9a71dShow Once: True
            • •
              294.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I wouldn't advise it. Play with fire and you get burned. That's always the way of it. [End]

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a8df6093-3e8b-ce65-8ece-1defcdcb683fSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h47b198d5g5a91g49fdgb2a6gedc4fc48efb9Text UUID: 0497590f-c32d-443a-9357-546bba78a840Timeline Phase Index: 202Timeline Dialog Node UUID: a8df6093-3e8b-ce65-8ece-1defcdcb683fTimeline Phase Duration: 7.87sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [2121.66s-2129.53s] | @ 2123.6s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [2121.66s-2129.53s] | @ 2125.41s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [2129.53s-2133.28s] | @ 2129.53s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)End Node: True
          • +
            192.Player: We should have as little to do with these tadpoles as we can.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 6f847b05-2c4c-4a6f-16c5-28e2932d9c64Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: h60a96187geca8g4a20ga579g1934c17d3419Text UUID: 40d99e69-72e0-46cf-b119-2e6b4e4bdd59Show Once: True
            • •
              29.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Well said. This dream figure is no friend to us. [End]

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 1134e410-256f-04df-fc13-d0c19db0e6a0Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hd0442f3agc2c1g42f6gb0e1gc84c46cf7ec2Text UUID: 88b464d3-36fe-45b4-8efd-8fb649579108Timeline Phase Index: 187Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 1134e410-256f-04df-fc13-d0c19db0e6a0Timeline Phase Duration: 4.68sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [2007.1s-2011.78s] | @ 2009.14s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [2007.1s-2011.78s] | @ 2010.84s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [2011.78s-2017.68s] | @ 2011.78s | speaker 0 | Surprise (64)End Node: True
          • +
            188.Player: Let's change the subject.

            Metadata

              54.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Good idea. [End]

    148.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I had another dream last night. The visitor came to me, and ordered me to penetrate the heart of the very cult that's spreading the infection.TG_Daisy_AfterDream2!TG_Daisy_AfterDream2

    Flags and Tags

    • +
      86.Origin_Intro_Wyll: It gave me a tadpole gift, too. Just like it did the first time it appeared. I suppose it hoped this would help.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 34bb71a0-6a5b-694f-f63e-bba8a7984a0dSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: heb619efbg5a62g4e84ga1e9g703a1153e153Text UUID: b5906746-7fad-4e24-b5fa-015469d4d20dGroup ID: 5a2a3d30-f4fb-226a-eb0e-80713a23d3a2Group Index: 1
      • +
        243.Origin_Intro_Wyll: At first I thought we should avoid these 'gifts', no matter what advantage we gain.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8cec6aeb-f578-6dc8-ce7c-e6d57060f76eSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h37ee595bg4bd8g4395g868dg1aac89abcbc6Text UUID: 693da04b-2a2c-46a3-b7d2-85781947eeb3Group ID: 5a2a3d30-f4fb-226a-eb0e-80713a23d3a2Group Index: 2
        • +
          366.Origin_Intro_Wyll: And yet... I can't help recall the words of my father. 'The best plan is the one that works.'

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: dcf7035c-bba6-381d-9ed4-628d5dc179d4Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h278526bbg7b93g4486gb346g7b8d5d70b2f2Text UUID: 7ada5d3e-443e-4641-8eb5-ccf268c2c434Group ID: 5a2a3d30-f4fb-226a-eb0e-80713a23d3a2Group Index: 3
          • +
            266.Origin_Intro_Wyll: These powers could be enough to edge us towards victory.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 981f23af-9a01-90d8-6aa7-5e0bf142812fSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h720be964gcca0g4f4cg893ega6d55ad661c2Text UUID: 9e0e673a-1660-40ba-a2f2-f2abe8a98207Group ID: 5a2a3d30-f4fb-226a-eb0e-80713a23d3a2Group Index: 4
            • +
              80.Player: We need these powers to infiltrate the cult. This is 'the plan that works'.

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 2f81c5ac-aa39-7a02-ea17-80c565d4db84Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hc5aab53bg2004g4d0fgbca1gecefd5bee990Text UUID: b8d270e3-a136-4365-8a90-b4de4d0f3756Show Once: True
              • •
                359.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Very well. If it's mind games these parasites wish to play, we'll play. And we'll win. [End]

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: d6f077b4-7f57-e443-2093-9bd582b9732eSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h37c1b989g2f7cg403eg9eaegdd47fd81f309Text UUID: a6b0b6e8-1717-4ccf-996d-52ea75772683Timeline Phase Index: 204Timeline Dialog Node UUID: d6f077b4-7f57-e443-2093-9bd582b9732eTimeline Phase Duration: 8.2sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [2133.28s-2141.48s] | @ 2135.1s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [2133.28s-2141.48s] | @ 2136.46s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [2133.28s-2141.48s] | @ 2138.28s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)End Node: True
            • +
              199.Player: I'd sooner avoid these powers if we can. I feel like we're being led into a trap.

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 7288f72e-4e0d-a117-ef9d-120a426d746aSpeaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: ha0cb0b42gcec0g4d4eg8825ga8731decda49Text UUID: a3631348-edea-4801-aac1-7000fb46b9aeShow Once: True
              • •
                137.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Then we'd do well to walk around it. Use these powers sparingly, if you must use them at all. [End]

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 52d09e15-0088-43a1-863b-d8e9098c6d1aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h61e9f92eg9281g469ega999g77e31f3028b6Text UUID: 716947d5-8c21-438b-b855-e791956875a6Timeline Phase Index: 195Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 52d09e15-0088-43a1-863b-d8e9098c6d1aTimeline Phase Duration: 6.55sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Emotion 1: Shot 1 [2080.15s-2086.7s] | @ 2082.45s | speaker 0 | Thinking (4)Timeline Emotion 2: Shot 1 [2080.15s-2086.7s] | @ 2084.92s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)Timeline Emotion 3: Shot 1 [2086.7s-2092.14s] | @ 2086.7s | speaker 0 | Fear (16)End Node: True
            • +

                150.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Sounds good to me. [End]

    170.Origin_Intro_Wyll: By Balduran's bones, this is a lot to take in. Let's see if I've got this right:TG_INT_EmperorRevealed_Event_SceneComplete!TG_INT_EmperorRevealed_Event_SceneComplete

      337.Origin_Intro_Wyll: One: We've been carrying a mind flayer around with us this whole time.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ca99db25-3029-7eba-29d1-46a8f76179fcSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h01acfae6g0807g4812ga819g7c1e5a627f0eText UUID: 1a514714-e6a6-4af1-a421-21d637997681Group ID: 67066473-1553-23a5-b6cf-f49fb26ef4a4Group Index: 1
      • +
        213.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Two: It's been appearing as someone else in our dreams, cajoling us to embrace our new illithid talents.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7c006adc-6320-90ff-d7c4-2e2a0742dfa1Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h607bae82g7421g4520g8a40g39b5aa46da2eText UUID: 9ef25acb-67b2-41f2-9656-2c78cdc8151eGroup ID: 67066473-1553-23a5-b6cf-f49fb26ef4a4Group Index: 2
        • +
          207.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Three: The mind flayer's been siphoning a githyanki prisoner's powers to shield us from the Absolute's voice.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7996ea92-f2d8-1c92-abe4-0a2a2e55d964Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h9b9bac76g98fdg4114ga2f5gab3736a41479Text UUID: d000500b-99b4-4fb9-9ffc-f68739ecfa06Group ID: 67066473-1553-23a5-b6cf-f49fb26ef4a4Group Index: 3
          • +
            307.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Did I miss anything?

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b64379f9-dfd2-2942-e4e6-a71ac8996760Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hf26c8074gecdcg4a34g8f6ega7ce29b16829Text UUID: cefae8c6-9100-4226-bc34-7438f09dad32Group ID: 67066473-1553-23a5-b6cf-f49fb26ef4a4Group Index: 4
            • +
              224.Player: He's just any githyanki. He's Mother Gith's son, living proof of Vlaakith's enduring lies.ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathLae'zel

              Flags and Tags

              checktype Global
              • ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath {97244ccc-a6b5-403e-9cfe-a3502eb4af56} = True
              checktype Tag | speaker 1 Player
              • Lae'zel {b5682d1d-c395-489c-9675-1f9b0c328ea5} = True

              Metadata

              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 827837c7-4ff2-c2af-0a16-5c6234fb0bd4Speaker: 1 (Player)Text Handle: hbaa65a38gb9c4g4e05gae11g7e2a0f23c54cText UUID: 1caf3234-bed9-4232-a8fe-54f1aa4155a5Show Once: True
              • +
                61.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Ah yes - the wayward prince, locked away by the queen who would wield his power.

                Metadata

                      43.Origin_Intro_Wyll: It holds our fates in its talons. Until the Absolute falls, there's no getting rid of it. [End]

                      Metadata

    400.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I fear we have no choice. What I ask is unreasonable by every standard. It sickens me to think, let alone say, but -!END_IllithidOptions_State_PartyHasOrphicHammerTG_END_NeedToBeMindFlayerForStones!Karlach!TG_END_NeedToBeMindFlayerForStones

      385.Origin_Intro_Wyll: If someone must assimilate Orpheus, I'd sooner trust you with the power than the illithid...

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: e8f06999-aeba-3c0b-0a6f-a06bbf1d77bdSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hd411efc0g56d4g4da5g89b5g546f06b92c88Text UUID: ee459202-9feb-49ab-869d-181fb4feacebGroup ID: f05a8b5d-d66a-90c0-66d7-996c4219503cGroup Index: 1
      • +
        153.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You'd become a mind flayer - an incredible sacrifice. If you refuse, I'd understand. Of course I would.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 5c432a53-e945-4e84-f9ff-6b00715b7cf9Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h020302b3gd760g4b49g96d6gfaf937c6e1eeText UUID: f92a57b3-73e6-400d-8715-34025e2b4c34Group ID: f05a8b5d-d66a-90c0-66d7-996c4219503cGroup Index: 2
        • +
          69.Origin_Intro_Wyll: That's where I stand. But whatever you do, I trust you to the last.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 2933b06d-c30a-c6d1-a4b1-4d1e1ac92219Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h9973462dg6c7cg44b8gbb31gc5e8933a757aText UUID: 2c26136d-8d09-400e-9416-778b091de9adGroup ID: f05a8b5d-d66a-90c0-66d7-996c4219503cGroup Index: 3

    • +
      28.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Death is staring you down. And so you want these last moments to count for something.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 10fa830b-bab1-55e0-a414-d84af8e86facSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h1d8448e0gb52cg43ddg8612geafac0dfb777Text UUID: fa9af8fa-d888-4799-b479-ece995138ebeGroup ID: f05222f4-dee2-4c4b-ab87-a7bf56834e5bGroup Index: 1
      • +
        74.Origin_Intro_Wyll: I can't bear to think of it. But I can't tell you 'no'.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 2bf9cc98-76d8-6ad2-5616-21546cb92ebaSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h93250460gb2a6g4c5bg8347gbb8e50479046Text UUID: 0fabbf0c-40d8-410d-8f09-a22e244a3c2eGroup ID: f05222f4-dee2-4c4b-ab87-a7bf56834e5bGroup Index: 2
        • +
          404.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You'd become a mind flayer - but you'd lead us to a sure victory. It's what you do.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: f4782424-2a07-c704-dd7a-1c165cae5bd1Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hf5b65402gc562g477cg8444g80737c8afc87Text UUID: 7c647102-a0fe-4e55-b1cd-62f640d0ab4fGroup ID: f05222f4-dee2-4c4b-ab87-a7bf56834e5bGroup Index: 3
          • +
            395.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But if assimilation is your choice, I won't stop you. I never would.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: efd7696c-08d7-48af-c34e-741160ab781aSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h1c3c69cbg4303g4fbdg87fbg5288a8f89dbdText UUID: 30b45c29-6ace-458b-abd1-a441e1ccf2b7Group ID: f05222f4-dee2-4c4b-ab87-a7bf56834e5bGroup Index: 4

    • +
      386.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Assimilate Orpheus for yourself, and you become illithid. The Emperor does it, and it claims the prince's power for himself.

      Metadata

      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ead31e85-7fb9-f7b5-c36c-a224740be2f2Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h84f44927g52f7g42dfg99abg2fc2031f2193Text UUID: 3d59d648-7b32-4200-9940-1fb64baf71adGroup ID: 803f27dc-63b3-30fc-5295-8164b46eec69Group Index: 1
      • +
        204.Origin_Intro_Wyll: And then there's the Orphic Hammer. We could refuse the Emperor entirely and break Orpheus free.

        Metadata

        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 76cb0960-111b-dbe1-bf4a-12bb0bad4ef1Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hfc3ddd09gb896g4fd1gb9e3g2ff3a95702e4Text UUID: a25d5586-8a7f-4ad9-bc01-c7638b9ae99aGroup ID: 803f27dc-63b3-30fc-5295-8164b46eec69Group Index: 2
        • +
          14.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Were I to choose, I'd break the prince free. Better to entrust our fates to him before making an unneeded sacrifice - or entrusting Orpheus' power to the illithid.

          Metadata

          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 09c47c6f-ecdf-7b05-b4f3-27edcb5838b4Speaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: hc33f888ega994g4b50gab8ag235af2769af4Text UUID: f6d2c8f5-6f6c-4acf-948e-e64f895a855dGroup ID: 803f27dc-63b3-30fc-5295-8164b46eec69Group Index: 3
          • +
            392.Origin_Intro_Wyll: But this is your decision to make. Wherever you lead, I will follow.

            Metadata

            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: eeef828e-abf7-de82-0a36-2fe44ed7efbfSpeaker: 0 (Origin_Intro_Wyll)Text Handle: h554f7c93gd32ag4757gb328g3beeec90b913Text UUID: c59523ed-eed3-489a-88cf-5c2b87d9b1edGroup ID: 803f27dc-63b3-30fc-5295-8164b46eec69Group Index: 4
            • •
